# @fluenti/next

> Next.js plugin for Fluenti — withFluenti config wrapper, webpack loader for t`` transforms, and server component generation.

## Overview

@fluenti/next integrates Fluenti into Next.js projects. It wraps next.config with a webpack loader that transforms `t`` template literals and `t()` calls at build time, and generates a server module for RSC i18n support.

- Package: @fluenti/next
- Docs: https://fluenti.dev
- Repository: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/next-plugin
- Peer dependencies: next >=14.0.0, react >=18.0.0
- Dependencies: @fluenti/core, @fluenti/react
- License: MIT

## Exports

Main entry (`@fluenti/next`):
- withFluenti(config?): Wrap next.config.ts with Fluenti support
- Types: WithFluentConfig, FluentProviderProps

Additional entries:
- `@fluenti/next/server`: Server-side utilities
- `@fluenti/next/provider`: FluentProvider async server component
- `@fluenti/next/client`: Client-side type definitions

## Quick Start

```ts
// next.config.ts
import { withFluenti } from '@fluenti/next'
export default withFluenti()({ reactStrictMode: true })
```

## withFluenti()

Two calling styles:
- Function style (recommended): `withFluenti(fluentConfig?)(nextConfig)`
- Direct style: `withFluenti(nextConfig)`

WithFluentConfig options:
- locales (string[]): Override fluenti.config.ts locales
- defaultLocale (string): Override sourceLocale
- compiledDir (string): Override compileOutDir
- serverModule (string): Custom serverModule path (skip auto-generation)
- serverModuleOutDir (string): Where to generate serverModule (default: node_modules/.fluenti)
- resolveLocale (() => string | Promise<string>): Resolve locale for Server Actions
- dateFormats, numberFormats, fallbackChain: Format overrides
